$comment: Shared Claude Code settings. The allowlist exists for unattended autopilot runs: a permission prompt mid-run can stall the agent for hours, so the whole ticket->branch->implement->PR->merge loop is pre-approved (read-only git/gh, tracker writes, branch/commit/push, PR create+merge, preflight, the library/research audits). Explicitly approved by the project owner. It deliberately omits gh repo edit/delete, gh api, and arbitrary shell. Trim entries to re-insert manual gates (e.g. remove 'gh pr merge' to approve merges yourself). The deny rules are tripwires for the policy-forbidden forms (PROJECT_CONVENTIONS.md > Merge policy); the prefix grammar cannot catch every flag position, so branch protection with 'include administrators' is the real guard. Hooks (both fail-open; see docs/AUTOMATION.md): block_naked_todos denies `git commit` while the staged diff adds a naked TODO/FIXME; session_start_banner injects Status-staleness + the open decision queue at session start and after compaction. Machine-specific grants belong in settings.local.json (gitignored).
